root_text.an4.7:1.1: “Cattārome, bhikkhave, viyattā vinītā visāradā bahussutā dhammadharā dhammānudhammappaṭipannā saṅghaṁ sobhenti.
root_text.an4.7:1.3: Bhikkhu, bhikkhave, viyatto vinīto visārado bahussuto dhammadharo dhammānudhammappaṭipanno saṅghaṁ sobheti.
root_text.an4.7:1.4: Bhikkhunī, bhikkhave, viyattā vinītā visāradā bahussutā dhammadharā dhammānudhammappaṭipannā saṅghaṁ sobheti.
root_text.an4.7:1.5: Upāsako, bhikkhave, viyatto vinīto visārado bahussuto dhammadharo dhammānudhammappaṭipanno saṅghaṁ sobheti.
root_text.an4.7:1.6: Upāsikā, bhikkhave, viyattā vinītā visāradā bahussutā dhammadharā dhammānudhammappaṭipannā saṅghaṁ sobheti.
root_text.an4.7:1.7: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cattāro viyattā vinītā visāradā bahussutā dhammadharā dhammānudhammappaṭipannā saṅghaṁ sobhentīti.
root_text.an4.7:2.1: Yo hoti viyatto ca visārado ca,

translation_text.an4.7:1.1: “Mendicants, these four who are competent, educated, assured, and learned, who have memorized the teachings and practice in line with the teaching, grace the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an4.7:1.7: These four who are competent, educated, assured, and learned, who have memorized the teachings and practice in line with the teaching, grace the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an4.7:2.2: learned, a memorizer of the teachings,
translation_text.an4.7:2.3: who lives in line with the teaching—
translation_text.an4.7:2.4: such a one is said to grace the Saṅgha.
translation_text.an4.7:3.6: they are the graces of the Saṅgha.”
